channel.title: NTU IoT Sensing Group
channel.description: Recent content on NTU IoT Sensing Group
channel.copyright: ©{year}, NTU IoT Sensing Group. All Rights Reserved

item.title: Sub Topic: Time
item.description: Powerline Electromagnetic Radiation for IoT Functions Sub topic: Time Wearables Clock Synchronization Using Skin Electric Potentials. Application-Layer Clock Synchronization for Wearables Using Skin Electric Potentials Induced by Powerline Radiation. Exploiting Electrical Grid for Accurate and Secure Clock Synchronization. Natural Timestamping Using Powerline Electromagnetic Radiation. Exploiting Power Grid for Accurate and Secure Clock Synchronization in Industrial IoT.

item.title: Duc
item.description: Duc Van Le Position: Research Fellow Contact: vdle@ntu.edu.sg Bio: Duc Van Le is research fellow at the HP-NTU Digital Manufacturing Corporate Lab, Nanyang Technological University (NTU), Singapore. He obtained his B.Eng. degree in electronics and telecommunications engineering from the Le Quy Don Technical University, Vietnam, and PhD degree in computer engineering from the University of Ulsan, South Korea, in 2011 and 2016, respectively. Before joining NTU, he worked as a research fellow at Department of Computer Science, National University of Singapore (NUS) from 2016 to 2018.

item.title: Zhenyu Yan
item.description: Zhenyu Yan Position: PhD Candidate Contact: zyan006@e.ntu.edu.sg Bio: Zhenyu Yan is a final year Ph.D. student in NTU IoT Sensing Group. He received his bachelor’s degrees in both Electronic Engineering and Finance from University of Electronic Science and Technology of China (UESTC). His research focuses on finding solutions for novel applications, security and privacy issues in IoT systems. Personal website and Publication: https://www.yanzhenyu.com

item.title: Sub Topic: Location
item.description: Powerline Electromagnetic Radiation for IoT Functions Sub topic: Location Towards Touch-to-Access Device Authentication Using Induced Body Electric Potentials. Simultaneous Localization and Mapping with Power Network Electromagnetic Field.

item.title: Chaojie Gu
item.description: Chaojie Gu Position: PhD Candidate, Project Officer Contact: gucj@ntu.edu.sg Bio: Chaojie Gu is a Ph.D. candidate at SCSE of NTU. He received his B.Eng. degree from Harbin Institute of Technology, China, in 2016. His research interests include IoT and Low-Power Wide Area Networks. Personal website: https://chaojiegu.github.io/ Publication: https://chaojiegu.github.io/publication/ Research projects:

item.title: Dongfang Guo
item.description: Dongfang Guo Position: Research Associate Contact: dongfang.guo@ntu.edu.sg Bio: I am currently a Research Associate in Prof. Tan’s research group. I received my B.Eng. degree from University of Electronic Science and Technology of China, and my M.Sc. degree from Nanyang Technological University, Singapore. My research interests lie in the field of Internet of Things and Wireless Sensor Networks. I am now digging into LoRa and exploring its application in indoor object localization.

item.title: Jiale Chen
item.description: Jiale Chen Position: Research Associate Contact: +65 88708195 (Mobile phone) jiale.chen@ntu.edu.sg Bio: I am now a research associate in HP/NTU Lab. I received my Master degree from Hong Kong University of Science and Technology in 2019. I got my bachelor degree from Southern China University of Technology in 2018.

item.title: Linshan Jiang
item.description: Linshan Jiang Position: PhD Candidate Contact: linshanjiangcn@gmail.com Bio: Linshan Jiang is a Ph.D. candidate at SCSE of NTU. He received his bachelor degree in Communication Engineering from Southern University of Science and Technology, China, in 2016. His research interests include secure and privacy in AIoT system, non-functional requirements of IoT. Publication: https://scholar.google.com.sg/citations?user=S01E5-cAAAAJ&hl=en

item.title: Alumni
item.description: Yingbo Liu (Research Fellow) Rutvij H. Jhaveri (Research Fellow) Hangtai Li (Visiting Student) Mengyao Zheng (Visiting Student) Dixing Xu (Visiting Student) Chongrong Fang (Visiting Student) Jue Tian (Visiting Student) Jothi Prasanna Shanmuga Sundaram (Visiting Student)

item.title: Qun Song
item.description: Qun Song Position: PhD Candidate Contact: song0167@ntu.edu.sg Bio: I am a second-year PhD candidate and my PhD program title is “Secure Machine Learning for Cyber-Physical Systems”. Specifically, my ongoing research focuses on constructing secure and efficient machine learning systems for edge devices and autonomous machines as well as power grid control systems. I am also interested in bringing machine learning to the edge. Personal website: https://sg.

item.title: Xu Liu
item.description: Xu Liu Position: Research Assitant Contact: x_liu@ntu.edu.sg Bio: Liu Xu obtained his Bachelor Degree from Nanyang Technological University in 2017. Prior to joined Prof Tan’s group he worked as a research assistant in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence Research Center. His current project is location sensing using EMR.

item.title: Wenjie Luo
item.description: Wenjie Luo Position: PhD Student Contact: Bio: Personal website: Publication: Research projects:

item.title: Rongrong Wang
item.description: Rongrong Wang Position: PhD Student, Research Associate Contact: Bio: Personal website: Publication: Research projects:

item.title: Siyuan Zhou
item.description: Siyuan Zhou Position: PhD Student, Research Associate Contact: siyuan.zhou@ntu.edu.sg Bio: I am currently a Ph.D student in school of computer science and engineering, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore. My currently research focuses on embedded deep visual sensing in industrial IoT. Research projects:

item.title: Asst. Prof. Rui Tan
item.description: Rui Tan Position: Assitant Professor Contact: tanrui@ntu.edu.sg Bio: Rui Tan (M’08-SM’18) is an Assistant Professor at School of Computer Science and Engineering, Nanyang Technological University, Singapore. Previously, he was a Research Scientist (2012-2015) and a Senior Research Scientist (2015) at Advanced Digital Sciences Center, a Singapore-based research center of University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign (UIUC), a Principle Research Affiliate (2012-2015) at Coordinated Science Lab of UIUC, and a postdoctoral Research Associate (2010-2012) at Department of Computer Science and Engineering, Michigan State University, East Lansing.
